violations.text: At the time of inspection, there was no on-site manager certified in food safety as required at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. (Employee Food Safety Certificate currently expired.) Employee to be registered for Food Safety Certification class and test as soon as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 03/10/2026

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The food disposal located under the pre-flush sink at the dish machine was observed to be clogging and not draining properly. Per maintenance staff, new unit is on site and due to be installed as soon as possible. - The high temp dish machine was observed to be losing wash cycle pressure while in continuous use (per the PIC, a workorder has been written and plans for repair/replacement are in process). PIC is to ensure all equipment/utensils are properly checked for thorough cleaning of food debris and rewashed if needed. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/08/2025

Cut Chicken Strips was observed held at 110-124 degrees Fahrenheit in the service line steam unit and hot hold cabinet (per the PIC, chicken cooled down during the prep process adding bbq sauce). Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was properly reheated at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

At the time of inspection, the following equipment was observed in need of repair/adjustment... - The food disposal located under the pre-flush sink at the dish machine was observed to be clogging and not draining properly. Per maintenance staff, new unit is on order and due to for replacement - The pressure indicator device for the high temp dish machine was observed at 38 psi and not at the required 15-25 psi as necessary with high temp dish machine sanitizing. Dish machine was observed properly sanitizing as indicated by thermo-label test strip. Pressure gauge to be replaced/repaired. - - The high temp dish machine was observed to be losing wash cycle pressure while in continuous use (per the PIC, a workorder is in place for service within 2 weeks of inspection). PIC is to ensure all equipment/utensils are properly check for thorough cleaning of food debris and rewashed if needed. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2025

At the time of inspection, a ceiling light shield over the food service line area was observed with dripping/pooling water (raining at time of inspection). At the time of inspection, water was NOT observed to be dripping from the light shield onto the food service line or affecting any foods or food safety. PIC to ensure no food is stored for service on the service line under or near any possible dripping water from the ceiling/light during rain, snow, and/or snow melt events until ceiling or roof is properly repaired and preventing any leaks. (Per maintenance staff, future plans are in place for future repairs) New Violation. Correct By: 09/25/2025

At the time of inspection, the flow pressure of the fresh hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) was not between 15-25 PSI as required (Observed at 40 PSI). School maintenance personnel was made aware at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

At the time of inspection, the "Closer" mechanism to the walk-in cooler door was observed to be in need of adjustment/repair/replacement. The Closing mechanism was observed to not pull to the door shut as intended, allowing for the door to remain ajar. Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

At the time of inspection, the drain to the pre-flush sink at the dish machine area was observed to be clogged/slowly draining. Per the PIC, the is a problem with the attached garbage disposal. Maintenance personnel was made aware at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

At the time of inspection, the flow pressure of the fresh hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) was not between 15-25 PSI as required. Based on the dial used for displaying the rinse cycle pressure, the flow pressure was observed to be 40 PSI. Maintenance was made aware of the pressure in need of adjustment at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024
The hand-washing sink in the dish washing area was observed to be lacking paper towels. Paper Towels were supplied at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

At the time of inspection, the flow pressure of the fresh hot water sanitizing rinse (PSI) was not between 15-25 PSI as required. Based on the dial used for displaying the rinse cycle pressure, the flow pressure was observed to be 40 PSI. Maintenance was made aware of the pressure in need of adjustment at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/21/2024

Chicken was observed held at 119 to 122 degrees Fahrenheit in the hot hold cabinet. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken was properly reheated to 165 at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024

Sliced Tomato, Cucumber, and Lettuce were observed held at 51 degrees Fahrenheit on the lunch service line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC to ensure proper temperature control of Time/Temperature Control for Safety Foods by displaying small portions held on ice with bulk portions to be kept in refrigeration between lunch periods. New Violation. Correct By: 03/07/2024
